generatedSentence: In the fifteenth cycle, a young prodigy felt deeply out of sync with the normality of mankind, often standing alone in his leatherjackets. His walkman and audiobooks were his only escape from the awfully stuffy laboratory where he studied an ancient veinstone object. The usual landscape of towering architecture seemed a mere break from the anomaly of his own wishful thinking, as he collected whispered words from an old economist about forgotten loans that shaped the nowadays world, often seen docking at the laundry station. The uproar began nocturnally when the regime kicked off a new, ideological campaign, fueled by the barking of propaganda machines. Under a perpetually smoking chimney, citizens in raincoats were subjected to backslapping enforcers, their kneepad protection doing little good against the rising tide of corruption. The 'effective' system was kaput, and a notorious jailbird named Rickey, known for his tribal affiliations and the mangos he offered as payoffs, introduced digital trojans, causing widespread grieving, pushing the empire uphill with sanctions. Suddenly, the object he’d studied, a fossilized dinosaur, began to glow, emitting a fiery light that outjumped the ambient falloff. The trickster's hardware, previously thought to be aftermarket junk, activated ultrafast fanjets and ramjets from the gundeck of his hidden unit. He whipped into action, dodging an incoming volley of energy blasts, his movements precise, answering the call with an abrupt, powerful surge, breaking the fragile grip of the old kingdom’s timer. Through the frostbite and icecream vendors, his odyssey continued underground, seeking to empower mankind, surviving the rigorous oncology of this new world. It was a groundbreaking campaign for justice, a verified step towards a better future, a handover from the mummy-like grasp of the past to his granddaughter. With justice done, the universe was no longer awkwardly aligned; he didn’t need to cheat to make things right, the same wish for justice now his kingdom’s anterior promise.
panelSentences: In the fifteenth cycle, a young prodigy felt deeply out of sync with the normality of mankind, often standing alone in his leatherjackets. His walkman and audiobooks were his only escape from the awfully stuffy laboratory where he studied an ancient veinstone object. The usual landscape of towering architecture seemed a mere break from the anomaly of his own wishful thinking, as he collected whispered words from an old economist about forgotten loans that shaped the nowadays world, often seen docking at the laundry station.

The uproar began nocturnally when the regime kicked off a new, ideological campaign, fueled by the barking of propaganda machines. Under a perpetually smoking chimney, citizens in raincoats were subjected to backslapping enforcers, their kneepad protection doing little good against the rising tide of corruption. The 'effective' system was kaput, and a notorious jailbird named Rickey, known for his tribal affiliations and the mangos he offered as payoffs, introduced digital trojans, causing widespread grieving, pushing the empire uphill with sanctions.

Suddenly, the object he’d studied, a fossilized dinosaur, began to glow, emitting a fiery light that outjumped the ambient falloff. The trickster's hardware, previously thought to be aftermarket junk, activated ultrafast fanjets and ramjets from the gundeck of his hidden unit. He whipped into action, dodging an incoming volley of energy blasts, his movements precise, answering the call with an abrupt, powerful surge, breaking the fragile grip of the old kingdom’s timer.

Through the frostbite and icecream vendors, his odyssey continued underground, seeking to empower mankind, surviving the rigorous oncology of this new world. It was a groundbreaking campaign for justice, a verified step towards a better future, a handover from the mummy-like grasp of the past to his granddaughter. With justice done, the universe was no longer awkwardly aligned; he didn’t need to cheat to make things right, the same wish for justice now his kingdom’s anterior promise.
rawPanelSentences: In the fifteenth cycle, a young prodigy felt deeply out of sync with the normality of mankind, often standing alone in his leatherjackets. His walkman and audiobooks were his only escape from the awfully stuffy laboratory where he studied an ancient veinstone object. The usual landscape of towering architecture seemed a mere break from the anomaly of his own wishful thinking, as he collected whispered words from an old economist about forgotten loans that shaped the nowadays world, often seen docking at the laundry station.

The uproar began nocturnally when the regime kicked off a new, ideological campaign, fueled by the barking of propaganda machines. Under a perpetually smoking chimney, citizens in raincoats were subjected to backslapping enforcers, their kneepad protection doing little good against the rising tide of corruption. The 'effective' system was kaput, and a notorious jailbird named Rickey, known for his tribal affiliations and the mangos he offered as payoffs, introduced digital trojans, causing widespread grieving, pushing the empire uphill with sanctions.

Suddenly, the object he’d studied, a fossilized dinosaur, began to glow, emitting a fiery light that outjumped the ambient falloff. The trickster's hardware, previously thought to be aftermarket junk, activated ultrafast fanjets and ramjets from the gundeck of his hidden unit. He whipped into action, dodging an incoming volley of energy blasts, his movements precise, answering the call with an abrupt, powerful surge, breaking the fragile grip of the old kingdom’s timer.

Through the frostbite and icecream vendors, his odyssey continued underground, seeking to empower mankind, surviving the rigorous oncology of this new world. It was a groundbreaking campaign for justice, a verified step towards a better future, a handover from the mummy-like grasp of the past to his granddaughter. With justice done, the universe was no longer awkwardly aligned; he didn’t need to cheat to make things right, the same wish for justice now his kingdom’s anterior promise.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. The protagonist is a lone figure, cloaked in a tattered trench coat with glowing cybernetic eyes and a rusted chrome arm. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist stands contemplatively at a futuristic port's docking bay, observing towering, dilapidated cyberpunk architecture under a cloudy twilight sky, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, wearing a kneepad, is confronted by two heavily armored enforcers in raincoats, their faces obscured by helmets, against a backdrop of glowing neon signs and a smoking chimney, close-up, dramatic low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, now in dynamic motion, activates jet propulsion from his back, dodging fiery energy blasts from off-panel, a glowing dinosaur artifact in his outstretched hand, wide shot, action pose. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, with a subtle smile, stands beside a young girl, her hand in his, overlooking a restored, brightly lit futuristic cityscape with icecream vendors and lush green spaces, serene wide shot, eye-level.

A change, particularly the end of a spell of persistent good or bad weather.
A large four-wheeled carriage, having a straight body and calash top, with the driver's seat in front and the footman's behind.
(equitation) A sharp bit or snaffle.
A short section of music, often between verses, in which some performers stop while others continue.
definitions.example: The fiddle break was amazing; it was a pity the singer came back in on the wrong note.

definitions.definition: The point in the musical scale at which a woodwind instrument is designed to overblow, that is, to move from its lower to its upper register.
definitions.example: Crossing the break smoothly is one of the first lessons the young clarinettist needs to master.

definitions.definition: Usu. plural An area along a river that features steep banks, bluffs, or gorges (e.g., Upper Missouri River Breaks National Monument, US).
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.
definitions.example: break a seal

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.
definitions.example: to break flax

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.
definitions.example: to break into a run or gallop

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.
To suspend the execution of a program during debugging so that the state of the program can be investigated.
A toy made of a soft material, usually stuffed with cotton, straw, beans, or other materials, and typically intended as a plaything for children.
Poorly ventilated; partially plugged.
definitions.example: I can’t smell very well today – I have a stuffy nose.

definitions.definition: Stout; mettlesome; resolute.
Angry and obstinate; sulky.
Boring, uninteresting, over-formal, pompous, very conventional.
definitions.example: The stuffy professor droned on as the class lost interest.

definitions.definition: Stout; sturdy.
A user of the Twitter microblogging service whose identity has been confirmed by Twitter.
Subject to positive verification.
definitions.example: Only verified cardholders are eligible for the discount.

definitions.definition: To substantiate or prove the truth of something
To confirm or test the truth or accuracy of something
To affirm something formally, under oath
A kind or helpful deed; an instance of voluntarily assisting (someone).
definitions.example: He did me a favor when he took the time to drive me home.

definitions.definition: Goodwill; benevolent regard.
definitions.example: She enjoyed the queen's favor.

definitions.definition: A small gift; a party favor.
definitions.example: A marriage favour is a bunch or knot of white ribbons or white flowers worn at a wedding.

definitions.definition: Mildness or mitigation of punishment; lenity.
The object of regard; person or thing favoured.
Appearance; look; countenance; face.
Partiality; bias.
A letter, a written communication.
Anything worn publicly as a pledge of a woman's favor.
(in the plural) Lovelocks.
To look upon fondly; to prefer.
To encourage, conduce to
To do a favor [noun sense 1] for; to show beneficence toward.
definitions.example: Would you favor us with a poetry reading?

definitions.definition: To treat with care.
definitions.example: Favoring your sore leg will only injure the other one.

definitions.definition: (including) To resemble, to look like (another person).
Of or relating to fire.
Burning or glowing.
Inflammable or easily ignited.
Having the colour of fire.
Hot or inflamed.
Tempestuous or emotionally volatile.
definitions.example: a fiery temper

definitions.definition: Spirited or filled with emotion.
A recording, usually made available for sale, of a reading of the full text or of an abridgement of a book.
A design or image that has been influenced by indigenous peoples; especially such a tattoo.
Of or relating to tribes.
definitions.example: social order through tribal law

definitions.definition: Based on or organized according to tribes.
definitions.example: a tribal society

definitions.definition: An uphill route.
Located up a slope or on a hill.
Going up a slope or a hill.
(by extension) Difficult or laborious.
Up a slope, towards higher ground.
(by extension) With difficulty.
To enthusiastically affirm or congratulate a person, especially by patting them on the back.
Action of the verb to backslap
An approval, by an authority, generally one that makes something valid.
definitions.example: The whalers had been operating in the contested waters off the island with sanction from the Japanese government.

definitions.definition: A penalty, punishment, or some coercive measure, intended to ensure compliance; especially one adopted by several nations, or by an international body.
definitions.example: The United States enacted a new round of sanctions against the apartheid regime of South Africa.

definitions.definition: A law, treaty, or contract, or a clause within a law, treaty, or contract, specifying any of the above.
To ratify; to make valid.
To give official authorization or approval to; to countenance.
To penalize (a State etc.) with sanctions.
A deviation from a rule or from what is regarded as normal; an outlier.
Something or someone that is strange or unusual.
definitions.example: He is an anomaly among his friends in that he's the only one who's unmarried.

definitions.definition: Any event or measurement that is out of the ordinary regardless of whether it is exceptional or not.
definitions.example: She disregarded some of the anamolies in the experiment, putting them down to miscalculation.

definitions.definition: Any of various angular distances.
A defect or malformation.
A failure of a classical symmetry due to quantum corrections.
An irregularity or disproportion.
The art and science of designing and managing the construction of buildings and other structures, particularly if they are well proportioned and decorated.
definitions.example: Architecture is the visual public expression of a culture’s achievements, values, and outlook. ― Max Roscoe, "How Your City Is Killing You With Ugliness"

definitions.definition: The profession of an architect.
Any particular style of building design.
Construction, in a more general sense; frame or structure; workmanship.
A unifying structure.
A specific model of a microchip or CPU.
definitions.example: The Intel architectures have more software written for them.

definitions.definition: The structure and design of a system or product.
definitions.example: The architecture of the company's billing system is designed to support its business goals.

definitions.definition: A tropical Asian fruit tree, Mangifera indica.
The fruit of the mango tree.
A pickled vegetable or fruit with a spicy stuffing; a vegetable or fruit which has been mangoed.
(chiefly southern Midwest US) A green bell pepper suitable for pickling.
A type of muskmelon, Cucumis melo.
Any of various hummingbirds of the genus Anthracothorax.
A yellow-orange color, like that of mango flesh.
A type of music and dance that originated in the West Indies (perhaps Trinidad), characterized by improvised lyrics on topical or broadly humorous subjects, often creating satire of current events.
To perform calypso.
A maze-like structure built by Daedalus in Knossos, containing the Minotaur.
Part of the inner ear.
Anything complicated and confusing, like a maze.
Any of various satyrine butterflies of the genus Neope.
To enclose in a labyrinth, or as though in a labyrinth.
To arrange in the form of a labyrinth.
To twist and wind, following a labyrinthine path.
To render lost and confused, as if in a labyrinth.
An exhibition of works from an extended period of an artist's activity.
Of, relating to, or contemplating the past.
Looking backwards.
Affecting or influencing past things; retroactive.
To jump better than; particularly higher than, or further than.
A cover of minute ice crystals on objects that are exposed to the air. Frost is formed by the same process as dew, except that the temperature of the frosted object is below freezing.
The cold weather that causes these ice crystals to form.
Coldness or insensibility; severity or rigidity of character.
The act of freezing; the congelation of water or other liquid.
A shade of white, like that of frost.
A disappointment; a cheat.
To cover with frost.
To become covered with frost.
To coat (something, e.g. a cake) with icing to resemble frost.
To anger or annoy.
definitions.example: I think the boss's decision frosted him a bit.

definitions.definition: To sharpen (the points of a horse's shoe) to prevent it from slipping on ice.
Short for mainline.
To mainly play a specific character, or side, during a game.
definitions.example: He mains the same character as me in that game.

definitions.definition: Of a road: to convert into a main or primary road.
That which is chief or principal; the chief or main portion; the bulk, the greater part, gross.
A large cable or pipe providing utility service to an area or a building, such as a water main or electric main.
Short for main course.
definitions.example: I had scampi and chips for my main and a slice of cheesecake for dessert.

definitions.definition: The high seas.
The mainland.
Short for mainsail.
(except in might and main) Force, power, strength, violent effort.
A hand or match in a game of dice.
The largest throw in a match at dice; in the game of hazard, a number from one to nine called out by a person before the dice are thrown.
A stake played for at dice.
A sporting contest or match, especially a cockfighting match.
A banker's shovel for coins.
A basket for gathering grapes.
The domestic electrical power supply.
definitions.example: I plugged it into the mains and it blew up!

definitions.definition: The pipes of a centralized water supply that transport the water to individual buildings.
The main course of a meal.
An act or instance of lending, an act or instance of granting something for temporary use.
definitions.example: Because of the loan that John made to me, I was able to pay my tuition for the upcoming semester.

definitions.definition: A sum of money or other property that a natural or legal person borrows from another with the condition that it be returned or repaid over time or at a later date (sometimes with interest).
definitions.example: All loans from the library, whether books or audio material, must be returned within two weeks.

definitions.definition: The contract and array of legal or ethical obligations surrounding a loan.
definitions.example: He made a payment on his loan.

definitions.definition: The permission to borrow any item.
definitions.example: Thank you for the loan of your lawn mower.

definitions.definition: To lend (something) to (someone).
A lonnen.
The note on which a musical key is based; the tonic.
The main theme of a speech, a written work, or a conference.
A speech that sets the main theme of a conference or other gathering; a keynote speech or keynote address.
To deliver a speech that sets the main theme of a conference or other gathering.
A room containing a public toilet: a public lavatory.
definitions.example: Could you tell me where I can find the restroom?

definitions.definition: In scientific usage, any of the animals belonging to the clade Dinosauria, especially those that existed during the Triassic, Jurassic and Cretaceous periods and are now extinct.
In non-scientific usage, any non-avian dinosaur.
Any extinct reptile, not necessarily belonging to Dinosauria, that existed between about 230 million and 65 million years ago.
A person or organisation that is very old, has very old-fashioned views, or is not willing to change and adapt.
Anything no longer in common use or practice.
A dessert made from frozen sweetened cream or a similar substance, usually flavoured.
definitions.example: Strawberries are particularly delicious with ice cream.

definitions.definition: A portion of ice cream on a stick, in a cup or in a wafer cone.
definitions.example: Can I have some money for an ice cream, Mum?

definitions.definition: In an awkward manner; with discomfort or lack of coordination.
The act of moving; a movement.
definitions.example: A slight move of the tiller, and the boat will go off course.

definitions.definition: An act for the attainment of an object; a step in the execution of a plan or purpose.
definitions.example: He made another move towards becoming a naturalized citizen.

definitions.definition: A formalized or practiced action used in athletics, dance, physical exercise, self-defense, hand-to-hand combat, etc.
definitions.example: She always gets spontaneous applause for that one move.

definitions.definition: The event of changing one's residence.
definitions.example: The move into my fiancé's house took two long days.

definitions.definition: A change in strategy.
definitions.example: I am worried about our boss's move.

definitions.definition: A transfer, a change from one employer to another.
The act of moving a token on a gameboard from one position to another according to the rules of the game.
definitions.example: If you roll a six, you can make two moves.

definitions.definition: To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.
definitions.example: A ship moves rapidly.

definitions.definition: To act; to take action; to begin to act
definitions.example: Come on guys, let's move: there's work to do!

definitions.definition: To change residence, for example from one house, town, or state, to another; to go and live at another place. See also move out and move in.
definitions.example: I decided to move to the country for a more peaceful life.

definitions.definition: (and other games) To change the place of a piece in accordance with the rules of the game.
definitions.example: My opponent's counter was moving much quicker round the board than mine.

definitions.definition: To cause to change place or posture in any manner; to set in motion; to carry, convey, draw, or push from one place to another
definitions.example: The horse moves a carriage.

definitions.definition: To transfer (a piece or man) from one space or position to another, according to the rules of the game
definitions.example: She moved the queen closer to the centre of the board.

definitions.definition: To excite to action by the presentation of motives; to rouse by representation, persuasion, or appeal; to influence.
definitions.example: This song moves me to dance.

definitions.definition: To arouse the feelings or passions of; especially, to excite to tenderness or compassion, to excite (for example, an emotion).
definitions.example: That book really moved me.

definitions.definition: To propose; to recommend; specifically, to propose formally for consideration and determination, in a deliberative assembly; to submit
definitions.example: I move to repeal the rule regarding obligatory school uniform.

definitions.definition: To mention; to raise (a question); to suggest (a course of action); to lodge (a complaint).
To incite, urge (someone to do something); to solicit (someone for or of an issue); to make a proposal to.
To apply to, as for aid.
To request an action from the court.
definitions.example: An attorney moved the court to issue a restraining order.

definitions.definition: To bow or salute upon meeting.
To sell, to market (especially, but not exclusively, illegal products)
A good ability to dance.
definitions.example: Check out the guy on the dancefloor, he's got seriously good moves.

definitions.definition: An underground railway, especially for mass transit of people in urban areas.
A train that runs on such an underground railway.
A rapid transit system, regardless of the elevation of its right of way.
An underground walkway, tunnel for pedestrians (called pedestrian underpass in US).
An underground route for pipes, sewers, etc.
Regions beneath the surface of the earth, both natural (eg. caves) and man-made (eg. mines).
(with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist political convention.
definitions.example: the French underground during World War II

definitions.definition: (with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist artistic convention.
To route electricity distribution cables underground
Below the ground; below the surface of the Earth.
definitions.example: There is an underground tunnel that takes you across the river.

definitions.definition: Hidden, furtive, secretive.
definitions.example: These criminals operate through an underground network.

definitions.definition: Outside the mainstream, especially unofficial and hidden from the authorities.
definitions.example: underground music

definitions.definition: Below the ground.
definitions.example: The tunnel goes underground at this point.

definitions.definition: Secretly.
An imitation of something, particularly a well-known product, usually lower in quality and price than the original.
definitions.example: It's not a name-brand bag; it's just a cheap knockoff.

definitions.definition: A device in a knitting machine to remove loops from the needles.
Not different or other; not another or others; not different as regards self; selfsame; identical.
definitions.example: Are you the same person who phoned me yesterday?

definitions.definition: Lacking variety from; indistinguishable.
definitions.example: It took all night to find our hotel room, as we forgot our room number and each door looked the same.

definitions.definition: Similar, alike.
definitions.example: You have the same hair I do!

definitions.definition: Used to express the unity of an object or person which has various different descriptions or qualities.
definitions.example: Round here it can be cloudy and sunny even in the same day.

definitions.definition: A reply of confirmation of identity.
The identical thing, ditto.
definitions.example: It's the same everywhere.

definitions.definition: Something similar, something of the identical type.
definitions.example: She's having apple pie? I'll have the same.   You two are just the same.

definitions.definition: It or them, without a connotation of similarity.
definitions.example: Light valve suspensions and films containing UV absorbers and light valves containing the same (US Patent 5,467,217)

definitions.definition: (Indian English, common) It or them, as above, meaning the last object mentioned, mainly as complement: on the same, for the same.
definitions.example: My picture/photography blog...kindly give me your reviews on the same.

definitions.definition: Indicates the speaker's strong approval or agreement with the previous material.
The daughter of someone's child.
From the interior toward the exterior; in an outward direction.
Outwardly; (merely) on the surface.
An extended adventurous voyage.
An intellectual or spiritual quest.
A turbofan engine.
An airplane powered by turbofan engines.
The transference of authority, control, power or knowledge from one agency to another, or from one state to another.
The information passed on in such a case.
definitions.example: The daytime team got an urgent handover from the afterhours department.

definitions.definition: (cellular telecommunications) the process of transferring an ongoing call or data session from one channel connected to the core network to another channel.
(satellite telecommunications) the process of transferring satellite control responsibility from one earth station to another without loss or interruption of service.
The transfer of goods from the dealer to the purchaser, often of illegal goods.
Before in place.
Before or earlier in time; prior to; preceding.
Nearer the forward end; nearer the head of an animal or the front of a human.
Mode of rule or management.
definitions.example: a prison regime

definitions.definition: A form of government, or the government in power.
definitions.example: a capitalist regime

definitions.definition: A period of rule.
A regulated system; a regimen.
definitions.example: Heaven will eliminate the tyrannical regimes.

definitions.definition: A division of a Mafia crime family, led by a caporegime.
A set of characteristics.
definitions.example: A typical annual water level regime would include a gradual summer drawdown beginning in early May.

definitions.definition: Fixtures, equipment, tools and devices used for general-purpose construction and repair of a structure or object. Also such equipment as sold as stock by a store of the same name, e.g. hardware store.
Equipment.
definitions.example: military hardware

definitions.definition: The part of a computer that is fixed and cannot be altered without replacement or physical modification; motherboard, expansion cards, etc. Compare software.
Electronic equipment.
Metal implements.
A firearm.
The market for further goods and services, such as replacement parts and accessories, subsequent to the sale of a product such as an automobile or computer.
The industry that serves that market.
Trading activity in a security immediately following its initial offering to the public.
To sell goods or services in the aftermarket.
To violate rules in order to gain advantage from a situation.
definitions.example: My brother flunked biology because he cheated on his mid-term.

definitions.definition: To be unfaithful to one's spouse or partner.
definitions.example: After he found out his wife cheated, he left her.

definitions.definition: To manage to avoid something even though it seemed unlikely.
definitions.example: He cheated death when his car collided with a moving train.

definitions.definition: To deceive; to fool; to trick.
definitions.example: He cheated his way into office.

definitions.definition: A realm having a king and/or queen as its actual or nominal sovereign.
A realm, region, or conceptual space where something is dominant.
definitions.example: the kingdom of the dead

definitions.definition: A rank in the classification of organisms, below domain and above phylum; a taxon at that rank (e.g. the plant kingdom, the animal kingdom).
At the present time; in the current era.
Easily broken or destroyed, and thus often of subtle or intricate structure.
definitions.example: He is a very fragile person and gets easily depressed.

definitions.definition: Feeling weak or easily disturbed as a result of illness.
An injury suffered as a result of freezing of some part of the body, typically fingers, toes or the nose.
To expose to the effect of frost, or a frosty air; to blight or nip with frost.
To engage in winter sailboating.
A response or reply; something said or done in reaction to a statement or question.
definitions.example: Her answer to his proposal was a slap in the face.

definitions.definition: A solution to a problem.
definitions.example: There is no simple answer to corruption.

definitions.definition: A document filed in response to a complaint, responding to each point raised in the complaint and raising counterpoints.
In an abrupt manner; without giving notice, or without the usual forms; suddenly; precipitously.
definitions.example: The professor stopped her lecture abruptly when she noticed someone fall off their seat.

definitions.definition: Any of numerous figures featuring in various mythologies and folk traditions, who use guile and secret knowledge to challenge authority and play tricks and pranks on others; any similar figure in literature.
One who plays tricks or pranks on others.
One who performs tricks (parts of a magician' act or entertaining difficult physical actions).
An impish or playful person.
A fraud (person who performs a trick for the purpose of unlawful gain).
To expel (one or more people) from their property; to force (one or more people) to move out.
